report_name: Ex-Ransomware Negotiator Sentenced to 70 Months for Acting as 'Double Agent' for BlackCat Gang

threat_actors.name: BlackCat
threat_actors.description: A notorious ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) gang that conspired with a corrupt negotiator, Angelo Martino, to extort higher ransom payments from victims in 2023.
threat_actors.goals: Extort money from victim organizations through ransomware attacks.

threat_actors.name: Angelo Martino
threat_actors.description: A former ransomware negotiator for DigitalMint who acted as a 'double agent' in 2023, providing his clients' confidential information to the BlackCat gang to help them extort higher ransom payments.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The BlackCat/Alphv ransomware gang encrypted victim files as the primary means of extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Org Information
attack_patterns.description: Angelo Martino, acting as a malicious insider, provided the BlackCat gang with confidential information about his clients, including their financial status and insurance coverage, to help the gang refine ransom demands.

malware.name: BlackCat
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the BlackCat/Alphv gang in their extortion operations, which were aided by corrupt negotiator Angelo Martino.

identities.name: DigitalMint
identities.description: The incident response firm that formerly employed Angelo Martino, the corrupt ransomware negotiator.

identities.name: U.S. Organizations
identities.description: A group of five U.S. companies targeted for extortion by the BlackCat gang with the help of insider Angelo Martino, with total ransom demands reaching $75.3 million.

identities.name: U.S. Department of Justice
identities.description: The government entity that prosecuted and secured the sentencing of Angelo Martino for his conspiracy with the BlackCat ransomware gang.
